prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_planning_subject student_candidate_1)
(reserve_specialization_slot_for_subject student_candidate_1 specialization_slot_1)
(enroll_subject_in_offering student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(confirm_subject_enrollment student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(reserve_advisor_for_subject student_candidate_1 advisor_slot_1)
(record_requirement_evidence_for_student student_candidate_1 requirement_category_1 course_offering_1)
(verify_student_requirement_with_advisor student_candidate_1 requirement_category_1 advisor_slot_1)
(release_advisor_from_subject student_candidate_1 advisor_slot_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(initialize_planning_subject cohort_candidate_1)
(reserve_specialization_slot_for_subject cohort_candidate_1 specialization_slot_2)
(enroll_subject_in_offering cohort_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(confirm_subject_enrollment cohort_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(reserve_advisor_for_subject cohort_candidate_1 advisor_slot_1)
(record_milestone_evidence_for_cohort cohort_candidate_1 milestone_checkpoint_1 course_offering_1)
(verify_cohort_milestone_with_advisor cohort_candidate_1 milestone_checkpoint_1 advisor_slot_1)
(release_advisor_from_subject cohort_candidate_1 advisor_slot_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering cohort_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(assemble_specialization_proposal student_candidate_1 cohort_candidate_1 requirement_category_1 milestone_checkpoint_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(enroll_subject_in_offering student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(lock_proposal_for_course_association specialization_proposal_1 student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(commit_specialization_to_student student_candidate_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(commit_specialization_to_cohort cohort_candidate_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(assign_external_partner_to_subject student_candidate_1 external_partner_1 course_offering_1)
(bind_external_partner_and_release_slot_for_student student_candidate_1 specialization_slot_1 external_partner_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering student_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(enroll_subject_in_offering cohort_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(assign_external_partner_to_subject cohort_candidate_1 external_partner_1 course_offering_1)
(bind_external_partner_and_release_slot_for_cohort cohort_candidate_1 specialization_slot_2 external_partner_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering cohort_candidate_1 course_offering_1)
(initialize_planning_subject learning_plan_1)
(reserve_specialization_slot_for_subject learning_plan_1 specialization_slot_1)
(enroll_subject_in_offering learning_plan_1 course_offering_1)
(confirm_subject_enrollment learning_plan_1 course_offering_1)
(register_supporting_document_for_proposal learning_plan_1 supporting_document_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(validate_document_and_mark_plan learning_plan_1 supporting_document_1 specialization_proposal_1 course_offering_1)
(reserve_credential_requirement_for_plan learning_plan_1 credential_requirement_1)
(reserve_capstone_for_plan learning_plan_1 capstone_project_1)
(reserve_advisor_for_subject learning_plan_1 advisor_slot_1)
(confirm_plan_credential_requirement learning_plan_1 credential_requirement_1 advisor_slot_1 supporting_document_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(release_advisor_from_subject learning_plan_1 advisor_slot_1)
(register_capstone_for_plan learning_plan_1 capstone_project_1 supporting_document_1 specialization_proposal_1)
(finalize_plan_signoff learning_plan_1)
(release_credential_requirement_from_plan learning_plan_1 credential_requirement_1)
(release_capstone_from_plan learning_plan_1 capstone_project_1)
(assign_external_partner_to_subject learning_plan_1 external_partner_1 course_offering_1)
(bind_external_partner_and_release_slot_for_plan learning_plan_1 specialization_slot_1 external_partner_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering learning_plan_1 course_offering_1)
(initialize_planning_subject learning_plan_2)
(reserve_specialization_slot_for_subject learning_plan_2 specialization_slot_1)
(enroll_subject_in_offering learning_plan_2 course_offering_1)
(confirm_subject_enrollment learning_plan_2 course_offering_1)
(register_supporting_document_for_proposal learning_plan_2 supporting_document_2 specialization_proposal_1)
(validate_document_and_mark_plan learning_plan_2 supporting_document_2 specialization_proposal_1 course_offering_1)
(reserve_credential_requirement_for_plan learning_plan_2 credential_requirement_1)
(reserve_capstone_for_plan learning_plan_2 capstone_project_1)
(reserve_advisor_for_subject learning_plan_2 advisor_slot_1)
(confirm_plan_credential_requirement learning_plan_2 credential_requirement_1 advisor_slot_1 supporting_document_2 specialization_proposal_1)
(release_advisor_from_subject learning_plan_2 advisor_slot_1)
(register_capstone_for_plan learning_plan_2 capstone_project_1 supporting_document_2 specialization_proposal_1)
(finalize_plan_signoff learning_plan_2)
(release_credential_requirement_from_plan learning_plan_2 credential_requirement_1)
(release_capstone_from_plan learning_plan_2 capstone_project_1)
(assign_external_partner_to_subject learning_plan_2 external_partner_1 course_offering_1)
(bind_external_partner_and_release_slot_for_plan learning_plan_2 specialization_slot_1 external_partner_1)
(withdraw_subject_from_offering learning_plan_2 course_offering_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves planning for specialization track choices with students, cohorts, learning plans, etc. The goal is to finalize external partner bindings for student_candidate_1, cohort_candidate_1, learning_plan_1, and learning_plan_2.


## Planning specialization tracks  
- The task centers on finalizing external partner bindings for student_candidate_1, cohort_candidate_1, and learning plans 1 and 2.  
- It involves coordinating student specialization choices with cohorts and learning plans to ensure alignment.

## Planning specialization tracks  
- To finalize external partner bindings, actions like binding external partners and releasing slots are needed for student_candidate_1, cohort_candidate_1, and learning plans 1 and 2.  
- Each binding requires the subject to be assigned an external partner, have a committed specialization, and be enrolled in course_offering_1, with no prior finalized binding.  

## Checking binding prerequisites  
- The binding process involves ensuring participant_external_partner_assigned, participant_assigned_specialization_slot, and participant_bound_to_external_partner for each subject.  
- Specialization commitment can be achieved through actions like finalizing plan sign-offs, committing to student, or cohort, with multiple paths available.  

## Exploring resource constraints  
- Only one external_partner_1 is available, but it can be reused after each binding action releases it.  
- Course_offering_1 is a shared resource, requiring subjects to be enrolled, which involves initializing and reserving slots first.

## Checking binding prerequisites  
- Finalizing bindings requires subjects to be ready for proposals, with students needing verified requirements and cohorts needing recorded milestone evidence.  
- Advisor slots, like advisor_slot_1, are consumed during verification but can be released and reused for multiple subjects.  

## Exploring proposal assembly  
- Assembling specialization proposals requires students and cohorts to be ready, with evidence recorded and a proposal token available, such as specialization_proposal_1.  
- The process involves locking the proposal with the course, ensuring the subject is enrolled and the proposal is reserved.  

## Planning resource sequencing  
- Shared resources like course_offering_1 and advisor slots need careful sequencing, with subjects withdrawing and reenrolling as needed.  
- Actions like binding external partners and releasing slots allow reusing external_partner_1 across student_candidate_1, cohort_candidate_1, and learning plans 1 and 2.

## Reviewing plan proposal processes  
- Plans require participant_ready_for_proposal, attached documents, and enrolled courses to register supporting documents for proposals.  
- Registering documents needs proposal_reserved and proposal_course_locked, achieved after assembling proposals for students and cohorts.  

## Exploring plan preparation steps  
- Plans need initialization, slot reservation, enrollment, and confirmation to reach participant_ready status before further actions.  
- Credential and capstone reservations are required for plans, with confirmation steps like registering capstones needing prior credential confirmation.  

## Checking elective bundle conditions  
- Elective bundles involve additional flags like proposal_requirement_elective_flag and elective_bundle_flag, but standard proposals avoid these for simplicity.  
- Using assemble_specialization_proposal without elective flags ensures evidence is recorded without elective fulfillment, simplifying the process.  

## Planning commitment and binding  
- After commitment, assign_external_partner_to_subject requires the subject to be committed, enrolled, and have an available partner, setting it as assigned and bound.  
- Binding actions like bind_..._and_release_slot finalize the process, releasing slots and partners for reuse across subjects.

## Examining plan proposal stages  
- Plans require participant_ready status, attached documents, and registered supporting documents to progress to stage1, avoiding elective flags for simplicity.  
- After stage1, credential requirements for plans need reservation, confirming availability and assigning credentials, ensuring no electives are involved.  

## Confirming credential and capstone steps  
- Credential confirmation for plans requires an assigned advisor, linked supporting proposals, and no milestone elective flags, leading to confirmed status.  
- Capstone reservation and registration for plans need prior credential confirmation, ensuring no elective flags and no admin package attachment for standard processes.  

## Analyzing commitment and enrollment needs  
- Commitment for students and cohorts happens after proposal locking, requiring readiness and satisfaction without needing plan involvement.  
- Plan finalization and external partner assignments require enrolled courses, with actions like slot reservation and binding needing careful sequencing to manage shared resources.  

## Managing supporting document logistics  
- Multiple supporting documents can be registered for a single proposal, consuming available slots but allowing validation for each plan's documents.  
- Document registration for plans requires proposal reservation and course locking, with advisor assignment needing sequencing due to limited availability.  

## Sequencing course enrollments  
- Course enrollments are crucial for actions like recording evidence and locking proposals, requiring plans to enroll one course at a time.  
- Actions like validating documents or committing credentials may not need enrollment, but external assignments and some initial steps do, necessitating withdrawals and reenrollments as needed.

## Sequencing resource releases  
- Releasing credential and capstone requirements from a plan, like plan1, makes them available for reuse by another plan, such as plan2, after finalizing signoff.  
- Slots and partners, initially limited, can be reused after binding actions, freeing resources for subsequent subjects like plan2.  

## Managing slot availability  
- With only three slots for four subjects, binding one subject frees a slot, allowing it to be reused for another, like plan2, after commitment.  
- Student and cohort can bind and release slots post-commitment, enabling plans to reserve needed slots without overlap.  

## Adjusting enrollment and binding  
- Student and cohort commitments don’t require enrollment, but actions like assign_external need reenrollment after withdrawal, impacting sequence.  
- Binding student and cohort early frees slots and partners, allowing plans to proceed while managing shared resources effectively.

## Finalizing plan processes  
- Plan finalization involves reserving advisors, confirming credentials, and registering capstones, ensuring all supporting documents are validated.  
- After finalizing signoff, plans like plan1 and plan2 release resources such as credentials and capstones, making them available for reuse.  

## Binding and withdrawing plans  
- Binding external partners to plans and releasing slots occurs after finalization, freeing resources for subsequent plans.  
- Plans are withdrawn from course offerings after binding, ensuring courses are available for the next enrollment cycle.  

## Managing advisor availability  
- Advisors are reserved for each plan during credential confirmation and released afterward, maintaining availability for other subjects.  
- This sequencing ensures advisors can support multiple plans without overlap, aligning with resource-sharing needs.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
